## 3. Deep Research panel

**Lifetime DR review verdicts**

| Verdict | n | of 412 |
|---|---:|---:|
| (no DR run) | 339 | 82.3% |
| ADJUSTED | 41 | 10.0% |
| OVERRIDDEN | 26 | 6.3% |
| ERROR_PARSING | 3 | 0.7% |
| UPGRADED | 1 | 0.2% |
| INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS | 1 | 0.2% |
| CONFIRMED | 1 | 0.2% |

Of the 73 rows that *did* reach DR: ADJUSTED 56.2% · OVERRIDDEN 35.6% · ERROR/INCOMPLETE 5.5% · CONFIRMED/UPGRADED 2.7%. DR's override rate on PM **BUY** specifically is **19/56 = 33.9%** lifetime; on **BUY_LIMIT** it is only **7/58 = 12.1%**, again largely driven by weak DR coverage of the BUY_LIMIT lane (see §6).

**Today's DR individual cases — NONE.** All 4 of today's PM verdicts skipped DR.

- APP (PM BUY_LIMIT) — no DR run yet. Worth noting AppLovin was **OVERRIDDEN → AVOID** on 2026-05-08 (id 356) with the prior DR citing "profound structural threats" — if DR is queued for today's APP it would be the most informative case of the day, given the reviewer has a recent strong opinion on this name.
- APPF (PM BUY_LIMIT) — no DR run yet.
- AVAV (PM AVOID) — DR not triggered for AVOIDs by current routing.
- AXON (PM PENDING) — never reached PM verdict, so DR not queued.

**DR funnel gap, last 14 days (BUY/BUY_LIMIT skipping DR): 29 rows** (24 BUY_LIMIT + 5 BUY). Lifetime: 42 BUY-tier rows have never received a DR verdict. The senior-reviewer is silently bypassed for ~half of recent BUY-tier candidates — same gap flagged yesterday, still uncorrected.

🚨 **WTW — stop breach now T+2 days without resolution.** Current price (249.61) remains *below* the stop_loss_price (253.50). Either a trailing-stop was raised above the current bar and the exit loop never fired, or the stop levels were inverted on entry (stop 253.50 > entry 248.99 is structurally suspect). Last reviewed 2026-05-11T22:09:33 — the desk-review loop has not run for **~40 hours**. **Escalating this to data-health §6** because it has now been visible across two consecutive audits.

No other ACTIVE position has <2% headroom; FLR (+5%) and MSI (+4.6%) are within striking distance of TP. CART/SHOP both made small reviews on 2026-05-11.

## 5. Today's full screening haul

Sector data is **NULL for all 4** of today's rows (`decision_points.sector` is unpopulated — see §6 MEDIUM flag), so groupings below are inferred from `company_name`.

All 4 candidates are tagged `gatekeeper_tier = DEEP_DIP`. Three of the four have `drop_type = MACRO_SELLOFF`, suggesting the screener saw a broad-market down day (AXON has NULL drop_type because it never finished PM).

**Software / vertical SaaS (2)**
- **APP** (AppLovin, ~$153B cap, −7.18%) → PM **BUY_LIMIT**, entry range 437.00–448.20 vs current 455.44. No DR. Note: this same ticker was **OVERRIDDEN → AVOID** by DR on 2026-05-08 with strong language ("profound structural threats"). Re-running DR is essential before any execution.
- **APPF** (AppFolio, ~$5.1B cap, −6.33%) → PM **BUY_LIMIT**, entry range 142.73–146.04 vs current 145.55. No DR.

**Defense / public-safety tech (2)**
- **AVAV** (AeroVironment, ~$7.9B cap, −6.05%) → PM **AVOID**. Routing-correct: PM rejected, DR not queued.
- **AXON** (Axon Enterprise, ~$29.7B cap, −6.30%) → **PENDING**, still unresolved. Most recent decision_point in the DB (id 415, 15:45). Worth checking by morning whether this is mid-flight or stalled.

| Outcome | Today |
|---|---:|
| Became BUY/BUY_LIMIT | 2 (APP, APPF) |
| Killed by DR | 0 |
| Became live position | 0 |
| Stuck PENDING | 1 (AXON — fresh, <24h) |
| Total candidates | 4 |

**Key observation for today:** the funnel produced 2 BUY-tier candidates and **zero** of them reached DR before close. If the senior reviewer's average historical BUY override rate (33.9% on BUY, 12.1% on BUY_LIMIT) is representative, ≈1-in-4 of today's BUYs would have been killed had DR run. This is exactly the coverage gap from §3 manifesting as a same-day execution risk.

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **The book did not move today.** No closes, no new entries, no DR overrides. With 4 candidates, only 2 reached BUY-tier, both skipped DR, and neither became a position. From a P&L perspective the day was a no-op; from a data-health perspective it's another tick of decay on the issues flagged yesterday.
- **WTW is now a two-day-old un-acted stop breach.** Yesterday's audit flagged this with a specific instruction to verify before next session. As of today's snapshot the position is still ACTIVE, the price is still below the stop, and the desk-review loop has not run since 2026-05-11. This is the most urgent operational item — every additional hour the system is asserting an exit condition it never enforced.
- **DR coverage on BUY_LIMIT is the load-bearing leak.** Today's APP (BUY_LIMIT) is the exact case the senior reviewer should be re-running: a name DR overrode just 5 days ago. The fact that the queueing rule didn't auto-route it back means the override does not persist across signals — every fresh PM verdict starts from zero. Worth confirming whether that's intentional.
- **Today's 4-row haul is small but not unprecedented.** 2026-05-07 also had 4. With a 14-day average of 15.7, days with single-digit haul are ~15% of trading days, so this alone isn't alarming. But coinciding with stalled background workers (`desk_reviews`, `decision_tracking`), it's worth a quick screener health check tomorrow.
- **Three highs of yesterday are unresolved.** WTW stop breach, empty `decision_tracking`, missing `ai_score`. None has shown evidence of repair in 24h. Either an engineer is on this and the report won't reflect it until tomorrow, or the fixes haven't been picked up.
- **The "Owned without desk_position" mismatch grew from 40 to 100** — that's likely the same population I miscounted yesterday (different join condition), not a real explosion, but worth confirming. If it really did grow by 60 rows in 24h there is a more aggressive writer leak than previously thought.
- **APP has the most informative DR pending of the day.** It is a name DR recently overrode with strong structural language; re-running the senior reviewer on the fresh dip would either confirm the prior overrule (high-information) or surface a regime change. Either result is more valuable than running DR on an untouched name.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions

Ordered by impact.

1. **Resolve WTW today** (HIGH, **repeated from 2026-05-12**). Either close the position (current is below stop) or correct the stop_loss_price. Audit all `desk_positions WHERE status='ACTIVE' AND current_price < stop_loss_price` and have the desk-review loop tick before next session.
2. **Restart / investigate the `desk_reviews` worker** (HIGH). Most recent run 2026-05-11T22:09. With 5 ACTIVE positions including a stop-breached one, the loop should have produced at least 5 reviews on both 05-12 and 05-13. Verify the schedule and the background thread is alive.
3. **Investigate the empty `decision_tracking` table** (HIGH, repeated). Blocks the backtesting harness. Confirm the tracking service is running and that this isn't a write-to-wrong-DB problem.
4. **Force a DR rerun on APP and APPF tonight** (HIGH). Both are BUY_LIMIT in today's haul, both skipped DR, and APP was overridden by DR 5 days ago. Either route them through the senior reviewer or document why fresh PM verdicts override prior DR opinions.
5. **Fix the queueing rule so BUY_LIMITs always reach DR** (HIGH). 24/39 last-14-day BUY_LIMITs skipped DR. This is the largest coverage gap in the pipeline.
6. **Clear stale PENDING rows** (HIGH, repeated). Now 28 rows > 24h, oldest 34.6 days. Re-run or mark ABANDONED. AXON (today) is the only fresh one.
7. **Restore `ai_score` writes** (HIGH, repeated). 0/192 in last 14 days. Either fix or formally deprecate the column.
8. **Delete the 9 `TEST*` / `T8_*` rows** (MEDIUM, repeated). They distort funnel rates and create phantom mismatches.
9. **Reconcile the "Owned with no `desk_positions`" mismatch** (MEDIUM). 100 rows is large enough to warrant a single SQL pass: for each, decide whether the decision_point's status flag is wrong or the position writer dropped the row.
10. **Re-mount the two canonical audit destinations** (MEDIUM). Two consecutive failed deliveries. Either update the scheduled task path or have the user re-create / re-share the folders so future runs land where they're meant to.
11. **Re-back-fill `sector`** (MEDIUM, repeated). 98.3% NULL across history.
12. **Re-run the GOLF INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS DR** (LOW, repeated). Single recent parser-incomplete case.
